other_observers: Nick Moore who agreed with ID
description: Walking along edge of marsh, feeding, neck held up and out like "normal" heron, not as is typical of BCNH. Neck looked longer than that of a BCNH, as did legs, imparting a rather different overall jizz. Bird was dark and grayish brown, grayer than BCNH. Underparts from throat to upper belly with more dark than light, as opposed to YCNH, which looks pale with brown streaks. Head more square, less elongated (hard for me to describe headshape differences between two species, but they do look somewhat different), than BCNH, with shorter and distinctly stouter bill. Face dull, darkish, no malar marking. Wing covert pattern showed dark feather center with narrow white edge on either side and small white spot at tip. Legs yellow-green.
